paper_title: SIMULATION OF CO2 INJECTION IN A DEPLETED GAS RESERVOIR: A CASE STUDY FOR UPPER MIOCENE SANDSTONE, NORTHERN CROATIA

methods.method_name: Analytical Estimation of CO2 Storage Capacity and OGIP
methods.description: This method uses published analytical techniques, specifically adapted from Lai et al. (2015), to estimate the theoretical CO2 storage capacity in a depleted gas reservoir and the original gas in place (OGIP) using production data. It involves applying the Material Balance Equation (MBE) and a specific formula for CO2 storage under certain assumptions, for comparison with simulated results.

steps.description: The material balance equation (MBE) is used for estimating the original gas in place (OGIP) from the available production data and average reservoir pressure.
steps.searchable_summary: Calculate Original Gas In Place (OGIP) using the Material Balance Equation (MBE).

steps.description: It was assumed that the hydrocarbon pore volume of the gas reservoir was unchanged during gas production and CO 2 injection.
steps.searchable_summary: Assume constant hydrocarbon pore volume during production and injection.

steps.description: ...and that reservoir volume of the OGIP should be equal to the reservoir volume of the mixture of remaining gas in the reservoir and injected CO 2 .
steps.searchable_summary: Assume reservoir volume of OGIP equals reservoir volume of remaining gas plus injected CO2 mixture.

steps.description: The liquid production was eliminated from the MBE...
steps.searchable_summary: Eliminate liquid production term from the Material Balance Equation for dry gas analysis.
outputs: MBE calculation approach for dry gas

steps.description: In this work, Equation 1 was used for calculating the theoretical amount of CO 2 that could be injected in the reservoir MALM.
steps.searchable_summary: Calculate theoretical CO2 injection amount using Equation 1.

steps.description: ...and the formula for CO 2 storage in the depleted dry gas reservoir was given (Lai et al., 2015).
steps.searchable_summary: Apply the formula for CO2 storage in depleted dry gas reservoirs from Lai et al. (2015).
inputs: Parameters required by the CO2 storage formula (from Lai et al., 2015)
